findings.name: IANA Time Zone Database
findings.detail: zone1970.tab lists Europe/Simferopol as 'RU,UA' — both countries, RU listed first. This is the dual-listing compromise documented in the IANA tz mailing list: when Crimea switched to Moscow time in 2014, maintainers added RU without removing UA.

findings.name: Google libphonenumber
findings.detail: libphonenumber has dual Crimean encoding: 3 entries under +380 (Ukraine, ITU-valid) AND 2 entries under +7 (Russia). Additionally, the +7-978 carrier file lists 4 Russian mobile operators operating under a prefix Russia unilaterally assigned in 2014 and never submitted to ITU. Every Android phone, Chrome sign-up form, and phone validation library worldwide treats the Russian +7-978 prefix as canonical.

key_findings: **IANA Time Zone Database** lists Europe/Simferopol with country codes 'RU,UA' in zone1970.tab. zone1970.tab lists Europe/Simferopol as 'RU,UA' — both countries, RU listed first. This is the dual-listing compromise documented in the IANA tz mailing list: when Crimea switched to Moscow time in 2014, maintainers added RU without removing UA.

**Google libphonenumber** has dual-encoded Crimean phone prefixes: 2 entries under +7 (Russia) and 3 under +380 (Ukraine). The +7-978 Crimean mobile prefix — unilaterally assigned by Russia in 2014 and never submitted to ITU — lists 4 carriers as active: Elemte-Invest, K-Telecom Ltd, MTS, Sevastopol TELECOM. Every Android phone and browser validation library that uses libphonenumber treats this unilateral prefix as canonical. This is the 'Standards Silencing' pattern: ITU formally lists +380-65x for Crimea, but the validation layer that every downstream application actually consults has switched to the Russian numbering.

**OpenStreetMap Nominatim** resolves 6/6 tested Crimean cities to country_code=UA (0 to RU). OSM applies the 'on the ground' rule but the Nominatim geocoder currently returns the Ukrainian classification.

The three probes measure different layers of the same underlying question: timezone databases (OS-level), phone number libraries (validation-level), and geocoding (application-level). The IANA and libphonenumber findings together establish that Crimean sovereignty has been quietly downgraded from 'UA' to 'UA+RU' or 'RU' in foundational infrastructure without any standards body or international regulator objecting or being notified.
limitations: Live fetches against github.com (IANA + libphonenumber) and nominatim.openstreetmap.org — results are snapshots at scan time.
Nominatim rate-limits at 1 req/sec; the 6-city test takes ~8 seconds.
libphonenumber changes slowly but not never — the +7-978 carrier list can gain or lose operators between scans.
The 'on the ground' rule in OpenStreetMap is under active discussion in OSM's WikiProject Crimea. The Nominatim result is a snapshot of the current OSM community consensus.
Additional infrastructure systems (CLDR territories, ICU locale data, Unicode regional codes) are not yet probed by this scan.
